calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Feb-06-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom: Lisa Fialco, Esq. for David C. Thomson (Petitioner).

The Court stated that it was not persuaded with the showing as to the joint tenancy, that it is not sure of the motivation which may be taxes, that the probate court does not view spousal property petitions for that purpose, that the purpose of a spousal property petition is set forth in the probate code, and that this can be done outside of probate with a declaration. Ms. Fialco argued in support of the petition stating that there are three different properties, that they seek confirmation as community property, that there is a presumption that it is community property, and noted that there is a tax benefit. The Court addressed Ms. Fialco stating that it does not agree with her interpretation, that it doesn't find the showing to be very strong, that it feels better with tenancy in common, and that it would give her that relief but not joint tenancy. Additional argument by Ms. Fialco is held.

At the conclusion of arguemnt, the petition for spousal property is taken under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
